document_title: Security of the EUDI Wallet Architecture — ENISA
short_description: ENISA analysis of security risks and mitigations for the European Digital Identity Wallet architecture. Identifies EUDI Wallet providers as high-impact targets for early PQC adoption. Covers security requirements for credential formats trust framework key management (WSCD/WSCA) and protocol security. Provides recommendations for member state EUDI Wallet implementations.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet Security; PQC Transition; Digital Identity Risk; WSCD; WSCA

document_title: Why quantum security is a question leaders cannot ignore
short_description: World Economic Forum perspective for C-suite and board audiences on quantum security strategic risk. Frames PQC migration as a board-level priority with competitive and regulatory dimensions. Part of WEF's ongoing series on quantum readiness for business leaders.

document_title: NSA Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Cryptography
short_description: NSA advisory explaining why it does not recommend QKD for national security systems. States that QKD addresses only the key distribution problem, requires special-purpose equipment, introduces trusted node vulnerabilities, and has side-channel risks in real implementations. Recommends PQC (CNSA 2.0 algorithms) as the primary solution. Key reference for balanced QKD vs PQC evaluation.
unresolved_reason: forbidden — NSA server blocks automated download

document_title: Use of Post-Quantum Algorithms in JOSE
short_description: Defines how to use ML-DSA (FIPS 204) and SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) as digital signature algorithms in JOSE JWS. Specifies alg identifiers ML-DSA-44, ML-DSA-65, ML-DSA-87, SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s and others for signing JWTs. The critical standard enabling PQC JWT signing across all JOSE implementations.

document_title: IEC 62443: Industrial Automation and Control Systems Security
short_description: Multi-part standard series for OT/ICS/SCADA cybersecurity covering security levels, zones, conduits, and lifecycle requirements. Foundation for SCADA PQC migration planning.

short_description: First single-trace side-channel attack on NTRU. Recovers the secret key from a single power trace, improving upon earlier differential power analysis attacks on NTRU implementations.

CA/B Forum Ballot SC-081v3: TLS Certificate Validity Reduction
short_description: Adopted CA/B Forum Server Certificate ballot reducing maximum TLS certificate validity: 200 days (March 2026), 100 days (March 2027), 47 days (March 2029). Domain validation reuse capped at 10 days at final stage. The primary CLM forcing function for CPM programs.

document_title: BIKE: Bit Flipping Key Encapsulation — Round 4 Specification
short_description: Specification of BIKE (Bit Flipping Key Encapsulation), a code-based KEM advanced to NIST Round 4 alongside Classic McEliece and HQC. Defines parameter sets BIKE-1, BIKE-3, BIKE-5 (NIST Levels 1/3/5).
authors_or_organization: BIKE Team (Aragon, Barreto, Bettaieb, Bidoux, Blazy, Deneuville, Gaborit, Gueron, Guneysu, Misoczki, Persichetti, Sendrier, Tillich, Vasseur, Zemor)
misc_info: Round 4 candidate (with Classic McEliece, HQC); not selected by NIST in March 2025 announcement (HQC chosen as 5th algorithm)

document_title: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD); Protocol and Data Format of REST-based Key Delivery API (ETSI GS QKD 014)
short_description: ETSI GS QKD 014 v2.1.1 standardises the REST-based Key Delivery API for Quantum Key Distribution networks. QKD is a separate quantum-safe technology from PQC — it derives keys from quantum-physical principles rather than computational hardness — but is often discussed alongside PQC as a complementary key-establishment option for ultra-high-security and government use cases.
authors_or_organization: ETSI Industry Specification Group on Quantum Key Distribution (ETSI ISG QKD)
SecurityLevels: Information-theoretic security (key establishment only — does not replace authentication or signatures)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Key delivery REST API; KME-SAE interface
ToolchainSupport: Multiple QKD vendors (Toshiba, ID Quantique, Qubitekk, etc.)
misc_info: QKD complements PQC for high-assurance key exchange but does NOT replace PQC for signatures, authentication, or general-purpose deployment. PQC remains the broader migration target per NSA CNSA 2.0.
unresolved_reason: version does not exist; use V1.1.1
